author: Elias Nikolaidis
msg:
The recent meltdowns have been a real disappointment. We are all here for the same goal.I really can't help but think we could channel our energy more effectively. What if we guided our collective efforts toward developing apps or brainstorming ideas to share and acquire more licenses?

Honestly, we will run out of licenses sooner or later. Our best path forward is to depend on each other—by pooling resources from fellow UNOs or sharing from within our own circle.

author: Calus B
msg: This thing is going to freaking blow us away

author: OC Nodes
msg:
I'm all for positive energy. I'm most joking. Although we could all use way more communication. A project this exciting and investment expensive deserves near daily updates from Dev and commercial team.. 

1- updates about task development 
2- updates about third party plug in apps
3- technical education about telecom tasks so we are all using this time to learn about this sector.. last Unity space they said "we assume you are all experts now in Telecom".. ?? I was taken aback honestly. Like what? We are here on a journey with you. Most of us are not IT and telecom engineers.. educate us and let us grow with you
4- updates about network growth.. daily.. how many licenses online.. where.. 

There are sooo many things they can be sharing. They can be with us here answering questions occasionally.. but no. Dead silence. I mean this is a verified UNO group.. so we are all motivated here together.. I still expect way better from Unity team

author: OC Nodes
msg: And that's amazing.. I can tell you me and three of my friends are waiting to buy another node. That's another 20k for the team.. but we need more comms

author: Unitynodehub.com
msg: Personally I am amazed at all the UNO giving away 90% to 70 % of the rewards.  UNO need a good return for the investment risk they took.   I am not doing less than 40/60.  I am thinking 50/50 should have been the only default on the market place.   It would have eliminated so much back and forth (race to the bottom) and focus more on uptime and network expansion.

author: Calus B
msg: Listen, they’re quiet because they’re working a ridiculous amount of hours. It’s tough I get it. I want this to come through. The guys they’re quiet as they’re working their ass off here. What’s coming is unbelievable. It is  quite literally not believable. It will be something you have to experience.

author: Calus B
msg: i’m tapped out. I can’t afford one more and I am doing everything I can in my power to buy one more.

author: G Boot
msg: You have healthy kidneys?? 😂

author: Gwailow
msg: I dont think it is surprising really - whilst supply > demand, the marketplace will always be a race to the bottom as its just a transaction with no relationship between UNO and ULO. So the only thing UNOs can compete on is price (i.e. high ULO %). Obviously where there is a relationship with the ULO, the UNO can get a better return as price is not the only lever. Letting ULOs have a taste of Unity with a low UNO return isnt a bad thing in these early stages (better than licenses sitting unused). All this changes as demand picks up (hopefully in line with rewards increasing).

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: You log in as a UNO, then you go to the settings tab (the most right one in the bottom. Then you go to "wallet settings" and set your split, lease duration, min uptime requirement and if your licenses are visible on the marketplace. Save and you are good.

author: Nico | NodeX
msg:
List of summary tasks I got from the AMAs...

🔍 Commercial Tasks Prepared
These include advanced telecom verification and quality checks such as:
Call route integrity checks
SMS delivery verification
Caller ID & Sender ID consistency
DLR (delivery receipt) reports
Telecom latency verification
Audio quality scoring (MOS-like testing)

🚀 Next Deployment Phase   (Scout & Runner?)

Telecom environment checks  
Multi-route comparisons across carriers
Increased utilization of SIM + Wi-Fi networks

🧠 New Technology & Task Families

Unity is also developing Wi-Fi network testing technology and has already prepared 10–15 new task families, including:
Multi-hop path analysis
Dual-device synchronized testing
Environment-based verification
Anomaly triangulation
Fraud pattern detection
ISP-level routing behavior analysis
Hybrid Wi-Fi & cellular tasks
Timing & sequence-based testing
Regional pattern verification
Deeper connectivity checks

author: T E
msg:
Might it be possible in the future to have a fixed amount for UNO per license?  For instance, a UNO earns the first $30/license (could be customized ($10, $20, $50 per license, but a flat amount).and anything above that would be paid to ULOs.  Obviously rewards would need to settle in a bit for that to make sense for both sides.      

Any increase in rewards would directly benefit ULO.  Would provide incentive for ULOs to engage in active tasks and provide income stability to UNO per license.

author: Dave
msg:
No, it's a proper calculation:

You paid 5k for 200 licenses including 8-9k each Wmtx and Mntx.
So you actually paid ~2k for these licenses, which comes down to 10 USD per license.

-> 25-35 USD per license is a 2.5-3.5x

author: Mat
msg:
The idea of a flat fiat amount is interesting but to start with let's K.I.S.S. and not confuse the market when they are learning. 
IMHO all focus & discussion should be on releasing passive tasks, showing us a roadmap and improving comms with the UNOs

author: T E
msg: I think it just adds greater flexibility for the UNO and the ULO. As a UNO, you could run some with a flat fee and others with percentage as well. Would shift the marketplace dynamics for sure.

author: Mat

author: Quoia75
msg:
I read it as having the capability to set a fixed earning amount so for example - $50 a month for ULOs. Or UNOs as the case would be. For me, I'd set a fixed earn for ULOs and the variable profit / loss would be handled on my end.

This would make advertising how much a ULO could make per license a lot simpler and it could also allow UNOs to set up earning tiers.

author: Quoia75
msg: Really looking forward to accessing better management tools via the web portal.👌

author: Nico | NodeX
msg: variable rate proposal is actually very interesting.

author: T E
msg: I think a fixed rate for ULOs could be interesting, but could be more complicated to set up. Less incentive for max uptime with a fixed reward and may only do the tasks up to what they would earn for themselves. Still, I think there would be a version of that that could work in situations.   Just a few more variables to consider.

author: Quoia75
msg:
Hmm - ok imagine this: A base fixed rate guaranteed as long as you meet the minimum uptime requirement. The incentive to meet the uptime is simple: if you don't meet it the license is auto-revoked. This base guaranteed earning would be sustained by the passive tasks. Extra earnings can be gained by opting into the interactive and third-party tasks.

Really would like having this feature. How difficult would it be to set it up? Don't have a clue. Access to a public API would allow people to whip up more bespoke experiences.

author: T E
msg: I mean, to that extent, we could link ourselves to a WM Network Builder and as part of their marketing ploy to get 1,000 users onboarded, we have them push Unity licenses which would allow them to offer customers a phone plan with better coverage for $19/month where $40 is actually just being paid for by the Unity earnings.  UNO could keep the rest.
